site_label: Near East and Europe pig domestication transect
exact_source_text: Pig domestication had begun by ~10,500 y before the present (BP) in the Near East, and mitochondrial DNA suggests that pigs arrived in Europe alongside farmers ~8,500 y BP.
interpretation_note: This is a broad domestication transect, not one excavation site, so the atlas point remains an inferred regional centroid.
